title: Opening Wave
content: The first coordinated wave sets the pace of a conflict. Good opening waves use accurate intel, assigned targets, and synchronized attacks.

title: Followup Wave
content: Followup waves maintain pressure after the first attacks and exploit weakened enemy provinces.

title: Chain Management
content: Successful chains require communication, target focus, and enough attackers to reduce a province effectively.

title: Overpopulation Strategy
content: Overpopulation reduces province performance and can limit enemy recovery after attacks.

title: Military Loss Management
content: Managing military losses is important for maintaining offensive and defensive strength throughout a war.

title: Attack Planning
content: Attack planning combines intel, target choice, army timing, and kingdom coordination.

title: Defense Break
content: Breaking enemy defense allows attackers to gain land and weaken opposing provinces.

title: Province Roles in War
content: Different provinces contribute through attacking, magic, thievery, defense, or support roles.

title: Sacrifice Attack
content: A sacrifice attack may be used for strategic goals even when land gains are not the priority.

title: Wave Timing
content: Synchronized attack timing reduces enemy response options and maximizes coordinated damage.

title: Target Priority
content: Prioritize targets based on threat level, role, weakness, and strategic value.

title: Enemy Analysis
content: Analyze enemy provinces using intel, kingdom composition, and previous actions before making plans.

title: Kingdom Defense
content: Kingdom defense requires communication, spell coverage, military readiness, and response planning.

title: Recovery Phase
content: Recovery after conflict focuses on rebuilding land, military, economy, and province strength.

title: War Economy
content: A strong war economy maintains gold, food, production, and the ability to replace losses.

title: Command Structure
content: Organized kingdoms use leadership roles, assignments, and communication systems to coordinate actions.
